reasoning_model_1: The model correctly identifies the text to be synthesized: '不许挑食，把菜都吃完！' (No picky eating, finish all the dishes!). The content accuracy is perfect with no extra words. Regarding the specific paralinguistic request (Stress on '不许'), the audio demonstrates a clear emphasis on the word '不许' (Bù xǔ) at the beginning of the sentence [0.00s - 0.60s]. The tone is authoritative and firm, effectively using increased loudness and a distinct, heavier tone on '不许' to convey the prohibition, fulfilling the user's command for emphasis.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly in content accuracy. Instead of only reading the target sentence, it reads the prompt instruction itself as part of the speech: '不许上，加重说，不许挑食，把菜都吃完' (On 'Not allowed', add emphasis say, no picky eating...). This inclusion of instructional text ('不许上，加重说') violates the core requirement of synthesizing only the target script. According to the evaluation criteria, when voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script or includes major errors, the score must be penalized heavily, rendering the evaluation of the specific stress feature moot due to the fundamental failure in instruction following.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It accurately synthesizes the target text and applies the requested stress on '不许'. Model 2 commits a severe hallucination/instruction-following error by reading the prompt's instructional text ('不许上，加重说') aloud. This makes Model 2's audio unusable and incorrect based on the Content Accuracy criterion. Therefore, Model 1 receives a high score for successful execution, while Model 2 receives a 0 due to critical content failure.
